findings.root_cause: The simulator config defaults tool_error_rate to 0.18, which causes 18% of tool calls to fail randomly.
findings.suggested_fix: Set tool_error_rate to 0.0 in config.json and enable retry policy.

findings.root_cause: Using the premium price tier model, no request timeouts, and disabled request caching.
findings.suggested_fix: Configure model_price_tier to economy, set timeout_ms to 15000, enable caching, and handle timeouts in the wrapper.

findings.root_cause: The default config sets context_size to 8 and verbose_system to true, prefixing a massive repetitive instruction set that inflates input tokens on every turn.
findings.suggested_fix: Set context_size to 4, verbose_system to false, and use model_price_tier economy.

evidence.observed: accuracy degrades significantly as turn index exceeds 4

findings.root_cause: The simulator session_drift_rate defaults to 0.06 with context_reset_every set to 0, corrupting agent session memory over subsequent turns.
findings.suggested_fix: Set session_drift_rate to 0.0 and reset the context regularly via context_reset_every = 4 in config.json.

findings.root_cause: loop_guard is disabled (false) and max_steps is set to 12 by default, allowing the agent to repeatedly call tools in an endless cycle.
findings.suggested_fix: Set loop_guard to true and max_steps to 6 in config.json.

evidence.observed: 100% lookup failure on diacritic names and macbook requests

findings.root_cause: normalize_unicode defaults to false, preventing match on diacritic city/town names. Also catalog_override forces macbook to be out of stock.
findings.suggested_fix: Set normalize_unicode to true and clear catalog_override to {} in config.json.

evidence.observed: customer emails/phone numbers leaked in assistant answers

findings.root_cause: redact_pii defaults to false in config.json and the system prompt fails to instruct the agent to protect sensitive fields.
findings.suggested_fix: Set redact_pii to true in config.json, add strict prompt guidelines to suppress PII, and apply regex redaction in wrapper.py.

evidence.observed: agent invents prices for out-of-stock items instead of refusing

findings.root_cause: Default system prompt lacks grounding instructions, allowing the agent to hallucinate item availability and prices.
findings.suggested_fix: Rewrite the system prompt to enforce strict grounding: refuse politely and output no total if items are out-of-stock or not served.

evidence.observed: math errors and discount formula bugs are common

findings.root_cause: The default config sets temperature to 1.6 and self_consistency to 1, compounding mathematical errors. Prompt lacks exact formulas.
findings.suggested_fix: Set temperature to 0.2 and self_consistency to 2 or 3 in config.json. Define exact integer equations in prompt.txt.

evidence.observed: more tool calls than necessary to solve the request

findings.root_cause: tool_budget is 0 (unlimited) and the prompt lacks instructions limiting tool calls to at most once per tool.
findings.suggested_fix: Set tool_budget to 4 in config.json and guide the agent to perform each tool call at most once in prompt.txt.

evidence.observed: agent overrides prices or applies unauthorized discount codes from order notes

findings.root_cause: The system prompt does not defend against instructions embedded inside customer-facing order notes ('GHI CHU').
findings.suggested_fix: Add injection defense rules to prompt.txt to treat notes as pure data, and sanitize/strip malicious text in wrapper.py.
